name: Geneva City Centre Traffic
description: Dense urban road network; high vehicle density; trams, buses, private vehicles
notes: Western terminus of City101; OSM: Pont du Mont-Blanc bridge centroid

name: SBB Rail Corridor – Geneva Cornavin
description: Main Geneva station; IC/IR/RE trains + Léman Express; shunting noise at night
notes: OSM: Place de Cornavin area; coordinate corrected from original estimate

name: Geneva Airport – Flight Operations
description: ~160 000 movements/yr; RWY05 departures overfly Bellevue/Genthod lakeshore at low altitude (~500m AGL)
notes: OSM: Route de l'Aéroport, Cointrin, Meyrin; dominant noise source western corridor

name: Autoroute A1 – Geneva to Nyon (mid-section)
location_name: A1 motorway between Geneva and Versoix
description: 6-lane motorway running 1–3 km inland; noise propagates to lakeshore especially near Versoix. OSM: Versoix municipality centroid used as reference
notes: Anti-noise barriers present in some sections; linear source

name: Geneva Airport – Ground Operations
description: Ground power units, jet engines at low thrust, baggage vehicles; same compound as record 3
notes: Largely contained within airport perimeter; coordinate shared with record 3 (same site)

name: Bellevue Lakeside – Aircraft Overflights
description: RWY05 departure path crosses directly over lakeshore; individual event peaks 75–80 dBA. OSM: Bellevue municipality centroid
notes: Critical noise hotspot on the lakeshore promenade; coordinate represents village centre

name: SBB Rail – Genthod / Bellevue Station
description: RE trains; track curvature generates squeal noise. OSM: Genthod municipality centroid (station not individually mapped in OSM)
notes: Coordinate represents municipality; station is near the lake at the western edge of village

name: N1 National Road – Versoix
description: National road running through Versoix village centre; heavy commercial vehicle presence. OSM: Route de Suisse in Versoix

name: SBB Rail – Coppet Station
description: IC/RE trains; significant linear source along the lakeshore corridor. OSM: Place de la Gare, Coppet

name: Coppet Marina – Boat Traffic
description: Outboard and inboard motorboats; CGN ferry calls; occasional jet-ski noise on open water. OSM: Port de Coppet marina
notes: Seasonal character; notable contrast to quieter off-season

name: Nyon Urban Traffic – Centre
description: Compact urban core with through-traffic on Route Suisse; market events. OSM: Place du Marché, Nyon

name: SBB Rail – Nyon Station
description: Major IC/IR stop; significant train frequency; station PA system. OSM: Parking Gare de Nyon node

name: Nyon Port – Motorboat & Ferry Traffic
description: CGN ferry, motorboats; harbour wall reflects engine noise toward promenade. OSM: Port de Nyon marina
notes: Seasonal

name: A1 Motorway – Nyon to Rolle (mid-section)
description: Motorway cuts inland from lake; noise propagates less to shore than western section. OSM: Gare de Gland area used as reference point

name: SBB Rail – Rolle Station
description: RE stop; lake immediately adjacent to tracks. OSM: Place de la Gare, Rolle
notes: Rail track runs ~50m from lake edge here

name: Rolle Port & Marina
description: Large sailing marina; mast clinking (aeolian) + motorboat exits. OSM: Port de Rolle (Rue du Port)
notes: Aeolian mast noise is characteristic acoustic feature; two marina basins verified in OSM

name: Allaman – Motorway Service Area
description: Motorway service area with truck parking; diesel idling at night. OSM: Allaman municipality centroid used (service area not individually mapped)
notes: 24h character; notable noise intrusion point

name: A1 Motorway – Allaman to Morges
description: Linear interpolation between verified Allaman and Morges points; motorway progressively approaches lake edge near Morges
notes: Coordinate estimated; OSM node not individually retrieved

name: Morges Urban Traffic
description: Anthropogenic – urban traffic
data_quality: Mid-size town with significant through-traffic on lakeside Route du Lac and centre streets. OSM: Place de la Gare, Morges
notes: Estimated / modelled

name: SBB Rail – Morges Station
description: High-frequency IC/IR/RE; busy intermediate station. OSM: Place de la Gare, Morges (station forecourt)
notes: Shared coordinate with record 19; station is within the same urban block

name: Morges Port & Lakeside Promenade
location_name: Port du Château / Place du Port
description: Large marina complex; lake festival noise events (Fête de la Tulipe etc.). OSM: Port du Château marina
notes: Event noise seasonal peaks

name: Morges Military Camp – Occasional Exercises
description: Military training area near Morges; intermittent impulsive noise from exercises. OSM camp not individually mapped; coordinate estimated from known location
notes: Low frequency; irregular pattern; coordinate approximate

name: A1 Motorway – Morges to Lausanne
description: Motorway runs close to lake edge; beach areas at Préverenges affected. OSM: Préverenges plage guidepost used as lakeshore reference

name: Préverenges Beach – Seasonal Recreation
description: Popular lakeshore beach; crowd noise, music from beach bars, jet-ski noise. OSM: Préverenges plage guidepost
notes: Seasonal summer peaks; shares location reference with record 23

name: EPFL Campus – Institutional Noise
description: Busy university campus; ventilation systems, vehicle traffic, outdoor gatherings. OSM: EPFL university polygon centroid

name: Lausanne-Ouchy Urban & A9 Noise
description: A9 motorway approaches lake; combined urban road noise intense at lakeside. OSM: Ouchy-Olympique metro halt

name: Lausanne Metro M2 – Ouchy Viaduct
description: M2 metro; steel guide wheels squeal on curves; viaduct amplifies noise toward port. OSM: Ouchy-Olympique railway halt node
notes: Coordinate shared with record 26 (same location)

name: Lausanne Ouchy – Port & CGN Terminal
description: CGN flagship hub; regular ferry calls; tourist crowd; port PA system. OSM: Port d'Ouchy marina polygon centroid

name: Lausanne Urban Core Traffic
description: Dense urban road network with trams; significant background noise over city core. OSM: Gare de Lausanne bus station node

name: SBB Rail – Lausanne Station
description: Major hub; IC/IR/TGV/EC/RE + shunting; one of the busiest Swiss stations. OSM: Gare de Lausanne node
notes: Noise propagates down to lake via Ouchy valley

name: A9 Motorway – Lausanne to Pully
description: Motorway runs above lake in cutting then viaduct; Pully lakeshore partly shielded. OSM: Pully municipality centroid

name: Lutry Village Traffic & Port
description: Lakeside village road; small marina; wine tourism traffic (Lavaux). OSM: Port de Lutry harbour polygon centroid

name: SBB Rail – Lavaux Lakeshore Corridor
description: Rail track runs directly at lake edge through Lavaux UNESCO vineyards; curved sections produce wheel squeal. OSM: Route de la Gare, Cully
notes: CRITICAL: major rail noise in UNESCO World Heritage landscape; key acoustic ecology conflict

name: Cully Marina & Village
location_name: Place de la Gare / Port de Cully
description: Wine festival events (Caves Ouvertes) create seasonal noise spikes; otherwise quiet. OSM: Place de la Gare, Cully area

name: Lavaux Vineyard Zone – Dominant Natural Soundscape
description: Reference quiet zone; wind through vines, birdsong, lake lapping. UNESCO World Heritage Landscape. OSM: AOC Lavaux–Villette vineyard polygon centroid
notes: Priority zone for acoustic ecology protection; lowest noise reference in corridor

name: Vevey Urban Traffic
description: Mid-size town; lakeside road dense with traffic; Nestlé HQ delivery traffic. OSM: Gare de Vevey bicycle rental node (station forecourt area)

name: SBB Rail – Vevey Station
description: RE/IR stop; GoldenPass connection interchange. OSM: Gare de Vevey node

name: Vevey Port & Festival Area
description: Vevey lakeside events area; Festival Images, Fête des Vignerons (decadal); outdoor concerts; CGN ferry. OSM: Port de Vevey marina polygon centroid
notes: Major event noise peaks; otherwise moderate

name: Montreux Urban Traffic & Lakeside
description: Tourist town with dense lakeside traffic; narrow valley amplifies road noise. OSM: Auditorium Stravinski area (lakeside central reference)

name: SBB Rail – Montreux Station
location_name: Gare de Montreux (Rue de la Gare)
description: RE + GoldenPass scenic line interchange; rack railway sounds (Rocher-de-Naye). OSM: Rue de la Gare, Montreux

name: Montreux Jazz Festival – Seasonal Event Peak
description: World-famous festival; outdoor stages + crowd noise on lakefront. OSM: Auditorium Stravinski building polygon centroid
notes: Isolated seasonal spike; not representative of baseline

name: A9 Motorway – Villeneuve approach
description: A9 descends into Rhône plain; motorway near lake edge south of Villeneuve. OSM: Zone industrielle de Villeneuve polygon centroid used as reference

name: SBB Rail – Villeneuve Station & Yard
description: Eastern terminus of main lakeside corridor; freight yard shunting adds low-freq impulsive noise. OSM: Place de la Gare, Villeneuve (VD)
notes: Eastern terminus anchor

name: Villeneuve Industrial Zone
description: Light to medium industry; logistics operations; HGV movements. OSM: Zone industrielle de Villeneuve polygon centroid (OSM ID: relation 19151963)
notes: Eastern terminus anchor noise source

name: Rhône Delta / Les Grangettes – Natural Soundscape
description: Protected wetland / Ramsar site east of Villeneuve. Very low anthropogenic noise. OSM: Chessel municipality centroid used as proxy (Les Grangettes not individually mapped in OSM)
notes: Priority zone for acoustic ecology conservation; natural baseline reference

name: Lake Surface – Wind & Wave Ambient
location_name: Lake Geneva open water – northern shore mid-corridor
description: Open water wind and wave noise forms background acoustic texture across entire corridor. Coordinate is mid-corridor lake surface estimate
notes: General reference value; not an OSM-verified point feature

name: CGN Ferry – Nyon to Yvoire crossing
description: CGN diesel-electric ferry; engine noise audible 200–400m from vessel at departure. Coordinate: Port de Nyon (OSM verified)

name: CGN Ferry – Rolle to Yvoire crossing
description: Seasonal ferry service; moderate noise footprint. Coordinate: Port de Rolle south marina (OSM verified)
notes: Seasonal

name: Versoix – Commercial / Light Industrial Strip
description: Retail and light industry area along N1; HVAC systems, delivery trucks. OSM: Versoix municipality centroid used as reference

name: Gland Station & Commercial Area
description: RE station; growing commuter town; bus interchange; parking lot noise. OSM: Gare de Gland (Route de Begnins bicycle rental node)
